title: arabis muralis subsp. collina
intro: Arabis muralis subsp. collina, also known as rock cress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of compounds such as glucosinolates and alkaloids. Rock cress has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, supporting respiratory health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies through infusion, decoction, or tincture, with the most common preparations being a tea or a topical cream for wound healing.
